title: Signup-Cohort Retention Matrix (W0-W8)

landmines.name: week-offset-year-normalisation
landmines.kills: naive/1_weekofyear_offset.sql -- computing the offset as (active_week_of_year - signup_week_of_year) with a year-agnostic week-of-year conflates the same week number across 2024 and 2025 and goes negative across the boundary, misassigning entire cohorts; the offset must be the difference of monotonic week indices.

landmines.name: internal-users-not-excluded
landmines.kills: naive/2_include_internal.sql -- leaving is_internal=1 users in inflates both cohort sizes and active counts; real retention excludes staff/QA/test users.

landmines.name: event-to-event-quadratic
landmines.kills: naive/3_correlated_slow.sql -- a correlated re-scan of events for every (cohort, offset) cell (equivalently an events-to-events self-join) is quadratic and TLE-slow at black scale; the reference pre-aggregates to a distinct per-user-per-week grid first (this is the designated naive-slow).

notes: Cohort = the Monday-anchored week of signup_ts (monotonic index from a literal calendar CTE, no reset at the 2024/2025 boundary). Per-user activity weeks are DISTINCT (user_id, week) so duplicate/double-fired events do not inflate activity (an active week is present or not, gaps and islands intact). week_offset = active_week_index - signup_week_index, kept for offsets 0..8. Every (cohort, offset in 0..8) cell is emitted via a CROSS JOIN of cohorts to a 0..8 offset spine LEFT JOINed to the counts, so an empty offset cell reads 0 rather than vanishing. cohort_size counts non-internal cohort users (W0 denominator); retention_rate = active_users / cohort_size, NULLIF-guarded. is_internal=1 users are excluded from both cohort_size and active_users. orderMatters=true, (cohort_week, week_offset) both non-null. naive/1 is the SQLite-dialect (strftime %W) representative of the raw week-of-year subtraction bug; its divergence is proven on the purple-scale fixture, naive/2 on the sample, naive/3 is TLE-only (identical output to the reference on the sample).
